info.name: Auth – Telegram OAuth (POST /api/v1/oauth/telegram/callback + PUT /oauth/telegram + DELETE /oauth/telegram)
info.description:
E2E tests for Telegram OAuth endpoints.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-oauth-telegram):

  Invocation 1 (single Newman run — JWT tokens must survive across folders):
    1. setup                — register gmail_test_email, fetch verification OTP, verify email, login → tg_access_token
    2. callback-happy-path  — new user callback (ID 99887766) → 201 + access_token body; same ID again → 200
    3. callback-failures    — invalid HMAC, auth_date 90 000 s old, auth_date 120 s in future, id=0, malformed JSON
    4. validation           — POST /callback body > 1 MiB → 413; PUT /oauth/telegram body > 1 MiB → 413
    5. link-happy-path      — PUT /oauth/telegram (ID 99887700) with tg_access_token → 204; verify via GET /profile/me/identities
    6. link-failures        — already linked (409), invalid HMAC (401), expired auth_date (401), malformed JSON (400)
    7. unlink-happy-path    — DELETE /oauth/telegram with tg_access_token → 204 (setup user has password; last-auth-method guard does not fire)
    8. unlink-failures      — provider_not_linked (404); last_auth_method (409) using Telegram-only user from callback-happy-path
    9. auth-failures        — PUT /oauth/telegram no Authorization → 401; DELETE /oauth/telegram no Authorization → 401
   10. rate-limiting-lnk   — dedicated +tgrl alias; rl-setup (register+verify+login); rl-link (ID 99887744 → 204, slot 1);
                              warmup 2-5 (already-linked 409, slots 2-5); 6th → 429 (Retry-After: 900)
   11. rate-limiting-unlk  — use tg_rl_access_token; warmup 1 (204, slot 1); warmup 2-5 (404, slots 2-5); 6th → 429 (Retry-After: 900)

  Invocation 2 (after Redis flush — fresh tgcb:ip: bucket):
   12. rate-limiting-cb    — 10 POST /callback with invalid HMAC from 127.0.0.1 (slots 1-10); 11th → 429 (Retry-After: 6)

NOT covered here and why:
  - Account locked → 423 (T-07): creating a locked account requires admin tooling not yet in E2E suite. Covered by handler_test.go.
  - Account inactive → 403 (T-08): same reason. Covered by handler_test.go.
  - Provider UID race / provider_uid_taken on new-user callback (T-10): requires concurrent requests; covered by handler_test.go.
  - Audit row content and metadata: covered by store_test.go.
  - context.WithoutCancel invariants: covered by service_test.go.
  - DB-level error injection: covered by integration tests.
  - Individual field-validation variants beyond id=0: covered by handler_test.go.
  - Refresh token cookie HttpOnly flag: covered by handler_test.go.
  - MintTokens failure path: covered by handler_test.go.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.
// rate-limiting-cb overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 in its folder prerequest.

item.name: setup
item.description:
Registers gmail_test_email, fetches the verification OTP from Gmail, verifies the email, and logs in. Stores tg_access_token for use by link/unlink folders.

This user has a password and no linked Telegram identity initially.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + tgEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching verification email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode verification email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in verification email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Verification OTP stored in tg_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] tg_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in register step did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] Setup access token stored in tg_access_token.');

item.name: callback-happy-path
item.description:
Verifies POST /oauth/telegram/callback for new user registration (201) and existing user login (200).

The prerequest script computes a valid Telegram HMAC using telegram_bot_token from the environment and CryptoJS (available in the Newman sandbox). Telegram ID 99887766 is used.

The 201 access_token is stored in tg_cb_access_token for the unlink-failures/last_auth_method test (that user has no password and cannot unlink their only auth method).

The second request re-uses the same ID — the identity row now exists, so ExistingUserPath returns 200.

exec: // data_check_string = sorted 'key=value' pairs (exclude 'hash'), joined by newline.
if (!botToken) { console.error('[ERROR] telegram_bot_token not set in environment.'); }
console.log('[INFO] HMAC computed for ID', tgId, '/ auth_date', authDate);
// NewUserPath: no existing user_identity for provider=telegram, ID=99887766.
// Creates user+identity+session in one TX, returns 201 with access_token body.
// Stores token in tg_cb_access_token for the last_auth_method test in unlink-failures.
pm.test('status is 201 (new Telegram user created)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));
console.log('[INFO] Telegram-only user access token stored in tg_cb_access_token.');
// Same tg_callback_id — identity row already exists. Recompute HMAC with fresh auth_date.
// ExistingUserPath: identity row for ID 99887766 now exists.
// Creates a new session and returns 200 with access_token.
pm.test('status is 200 (existing Telegram user login)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: callback-failures
item.description:
Verifies all error guards in POST /oauth/telegram/callback that can be exercised without admin tooling.

NOT covered here:
  - Account locked → 423 (T-07): requires admin tooling. Covered by handler_test.go.
  - Account inactive → 403 (T-08): same reason. Covered by handler_test.go.
  - provider_uid_taken concurrent race: covered by handler_test.go.

exec: // VerifyHMAC fires before any DB read (D-07). A tampered hash is rejected with 401.
// This confirms the HMAC guard is the first check after JSON decoding.
// auth_date is 90 000 s in the past (> 86 400 s replay window, D-09).
// Compute a valid HMAC so the HMAC guard passes, then CheckAuthDate fires.
// HMAC is valid; CheckAuthDate rejects the 90 000 s old timestamp.
// auth_date is 120 s ahead of server time (> 60 s clock-skew tolerance, D-09).
// Valid HMAC so HMAC guard passes; then CheckAuthDate rejects it.
// Both stale and future auth_date map to ErrTelegramAuthDateExpired → same error code.
// id=0 fails struct validation before HMAC; the handler returns 422.
// DecodeJSON fails on a non-JSON body; returns 400 before any HMAC or DB check.

item.name: validation
item.description: Verifies that MaxBytesReader rejects oversized request bodies with 413 for both POST /oauth/telegram/callback and PUT /oauth/telegram.

exec: // MaxBytesReader enforces a 1 MiB limit; handler never reaches HMAC verification.
// POST /link has the same MaxBytesReader guard; 413 before JWT or HMAC.

item.name: link-happy-path
item.description: Links Telegram ID 99887700 to the setup user (tg_access_token). PUT /oauth/telegram returns 204. Then GET /profile/me/identities confirms the identity row was written.

exec: // tg_link_id (99887700) is distinct from tg_callback_id (99887766) to avoid conflicts.
// JWTAuth passes, HMAC valid, auth_date fresh, no existing Telegram identity for setup user.
// InsertIdentity TX succeeds. POST /link returns 204 with no response body.
// Confirms the user_identities row was written by POST /link.
pm.test('telegram identity present in identities list', () => pm.expect(tgIdentity).to.not.be.undefined);
// Setup user already has Telegram ID 99887700 linked (from link-happy-path).
// GetIdentityByUserAndProvider finds an existing telegram row → ErrProviderAlreadyLinked → 409.
// VerifyHMAC fires before any DB read. Wrong hash → 401 invalid_signature.
// HMAC passes; CheckAuthDate rejects the stale timestamp.

item.name: unlink-happy-path
item.description: Verifies DELETE /oauth/telegram succeeds for the setup user (password present → last-auth-method guard does not fire). After this the setup user has no Telegram identity, exercised by provider_not_linked in unlink-failures.

exec: // password_hash IS NOT NULL → hasOtherAuthMethod = true → last-auth-method guard passes.
// DeleteIdentityTx removes the telegram row. Returns 204 with no body.

item.name: unlink-failures
item.description:
Verifies guard paths for DELETE /oauth/telegram.

provider_not_linked: setup user's Telegram identity was removed by unlink-happy-path.

last_auth_method: the Telegram-only user (ID 99887766) from callback-happy-path has no password and no other auth method. Uses tg_cb_access_token.

exec: // GetIdentitiesByUser returns no telegram row (unlinked by unlink-happy-path).
console.error('[ERROR] tg_cb_access_token is empty — callback-happy-path first request did not capture it.');
// User was created by POST /callback (ID 99887766): no email, no password.
// GetIdentitiesWithPassword: has_telegram=true, has_password=false, no other identities.

item.name: auth-failures
item.description: Verifies that PUT /oauth/telegram and DELETE /oauth/telegram reject requests without a valid Authorization header. Both endpoints sit inside the JWTAuth middleware group.

exec: // JWTAuth middleware rejects before the handler or rate limiter runs.
// JWTAuth middleware rejects before the handler or rate limiter runs.

item.name: rate-limiting-lnk
item.description:
Verifies the tglnk:usr: user rate limiter for PUT /oauth/telegram (burst=5, rate=5/15 min, Retry-After=900 s).

Why a dedicated +tgrl user: tglnk:usr: is user-keyed. link-happy-path and link-failures consumed slots from tg_access_token. To guarantee a clean bucket, this folder registers a dedicated +tgrl alias.

Sequence:
  rl-setup 1-3: register +tgrl → verify email (Gmail OTP) → login → tg_rl_access_token
  rl-link:      PUT /oauth/telegram ID 99887744 → 204 (slot 1)
  warmup 2-5:   PUT /oauth/telegram same ID → 409 provider_already_linked (slots 2-5 still charged)
  6th request → 429 (Retry-After: 900)

exec: if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for +tgrl verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + rlEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +tgrl verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +tgrl email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +tgrl email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] +tgrl OTP stored in tg_rl_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] tg_rl_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in rl-setup 1/3 did not complete.');
console.log('[INFO] RL access token stored in tg_rl_access_token.');
// tg_rl_id (99887744) — third Telegram ID used only by the RL user.
// Fresh tglnk:usr: bucket for the RL user. Slot 1 consumed.
// Returns 409 provider_already_linked; tglnk:usr: still charges slot 2.
pm.test('not rate-limited yet (tglnk:usr: slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// burst=5 exhausted. Retry-After = 900 s (tglnk:usr: window = 15 min).

item.name: rate-limiting-unlk
item.description:
Verifies the tgunlk:usr: user rate limiter for DELETE /oauth/telegram (burst=5, rate=5/15 min, Retry-After=900 s).

Uses tg_rl_access_token. After rate-limiting-lnk, the RL user still has Telegram ID 99887744 linked.

Sequence:
  warmup 1/5: DELETE /oauth/telegram → 204 (Telegram unlinked, tgunlk:usr: slot 1 consumed)
  warmup 2-5: DELETE /oauth/telegram → 404 provider_not_linked (slots 2-5; 404 still charges the limiter)
  6th request → 429 (Retry-After: 900)

exec: // RL user has Telegram 99887744 linked from rl-link. Unlink succeeds, slot 1 consumed.
pm.test('not rate-limited yet (tgunlk:usr: slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// burst=5 exhausted. Retry-After = 900 s (tgunlk:usr: window = 15 min).

item.name: rate-limiting-cb
item.description:
Verifies the tgcb:ip: IP rate limiter for POST /oauth/telegram/callback (burst=10, rate=10/1 min, Retry-After=60 s).

All requests pin _xff to 127.0.0.1 (folder prerequest override) so all hits land on the same IP bucket.
Run in a SEPARATE Newman invocation after Redis flush so the tgcb:ip: bucket is empty.
Warmup requests use an invalid HMAC (→ 401) but the rate limiter fires before the handler, consuming slots regardless.

Retry-After = 60 s (tgcb:ip: window = 1 min).

exec: // Override _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests hit the same IP bucket.
// tgcb:ip: IP-keyed, 127.0.0.1. Invalid HMAC → 401 but slot 1 consumed.

item.name: warmup 10/10 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (tgcb:ip: slot 10 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// burst=10 exhausted. Retry-After = 6 s = ceil(1 / (10/60)): time to earn one token at rate=10/60 t/s.

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique 10.x.x.x IP by the collection prerequest. Overridden to 127.0.0.1 by rate-limiting-cb folder prerequest.
~1 MiB padding string set in validation folder prerequest. Used to build oversized request bodies.
Set from gmail_test_email in setup/register prerequest.
Setup user password (TgS3tupP@ss1!).
Verification OTP for tg_email fetched from Gmail.
JWT for tg_email from setup/login. Used by link/unlink folders.
JWT for the Telegram-only user (ID 99887766) created by callback-happy-path. Used by unlink-failures/last_auth_method.
Telegram user ID for callback-happy-path. New user on first call, returning user on second.
Telegram user ID for link-happy-path and link-failures. Distinct from tg_callback_id.
Telegram user ID for the rate-limiting-lnk RL user. Distinct from both other IDs.
Computed Unix timestamp (seconds) for callback requests.
Computed HMAC for callback-happy-path requests.
Stale auth_date (90 000 s old) for auth_date_expired tests.
Valid HMAC for tg_stale_date.
Future auth_date (120 s ahead) for the future-timestamp expired test.
Valid HMAC for tg_future_date.
Computed in link-happy-path and link-failures prerequest scripts.
Computed HMAC for link requests.
Stale auth_date for auth_date_expired test in link-failures.
Valid HMAC for tg_link_stale_date.
gmail_test_email+tgrl alias. Set in rate-limiting-lnk rl-setup 1/3.
Password for tg_rl_email (TgRlP@ss1!).
Verification OTP for tg_rl_email fetched from Gmail.
JWT for tg_rl_email. Used by rate-limiting-lnk warmups and rate-limiting-unlk.
Computed in rate-limiting-lnk prerequest scripts.
Computed HMAC for rate-limiting-lnk requests.
